# SL-INDEX-010: critical-assets-blocked-by-robots

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

Critical rendered assets appear blocked by robots.txt.

## Metadata

- Category: Indexability (indexability)
- Default severity: warning
- Confidence: heuristic
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- robots-txt
- rendered-dom

## Required Evidence

- asset-url
- robots-rule

## When This Triggers

Identify critical linked assets and match them against robots rules.

## False Positives

This rule is heuristic. Treat the diagnostic as a review prompt and use DSL
severity overrides or suppressions when project policy intentionally differs.

## How To Fix

Allow crawling of critical render assets when they affect indexable content.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/indexability/blocked-assets

Fixed direction:

- Allow crawling of critical render assets when they affect indexable content.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
  rule through the common core engine.
- Crawler snapshots can provide crawl, robots.txt, sitemap, and site-graph
  evidence.
